items.description: More than 1,000 firefighters are battling two blazes in northern Japan, with some 3,000 people urged to leave their homes. The fires come as the country's winters become increasingly dry.

items.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
items.description: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

items.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
items.description: While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time — counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: China acquired capacity to build 50 nuclear power reactors simultaneously: Official report
items.description: China’s total installed nuclear power capacity has reached 125 million kilowatts, ranking first globally, State-run CCTV said, quoting the China Nuclear Energy Association report

items.title: Iran war: Trump cancels envoys' trip to Islamabad
items.description: Donald Trump said that he didn't want US officials to "sit around talking about nothing" in Islamabad. It comes after Iran said that there would be no direct talks with the US. More on DW.

items.title: News24 | Three Iranian tankers intercepted as US adds pressure in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The US military has intercepted at least three Iranian-flagged tankers in ‌Asian waters and is redirecting them away from their positions near India, Malaysia and Sri Lanka, shipping and security sources said.

items.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
items.description: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

items.title: First female Archbishop of Canterbury to meet Pope Leo
items.description: The 63-year-old former nurse, who is married with two children, is the first woman to lead the mother church of the world's 85-million strong Anglican community

items.title: Daniel Noboa’s ‘new Ecuador’ has some fearing loss of civil freedoms
items.description: Ecuador's Daniel Noboa appears to be consolidating power in ways that are causing growing unease in civil society. One week it’s political parties, the next it’s the courts. Now it’s the media. Leer más

items.title: Bangladesh issues nationwide security alert over possible militant attacks
items.description: "The alert has been issued based on intelligence reports,” a police headquarters (PHQ) official said, requesting anonymity. The official described the alert as “urgent and confidential”

items.title: Kenyan appeal court overturns ruling that affirmed the right to abortion
items.description: A court of appeal in Kenya Friday overturned a ruling that affirmed the right to access abortion, setting up a legal clash that is likely to be appealed again to the Kenyan Supreme Court.

items.title: Data center provider Csquare confidentially files for US IPO
Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners
items.description: A Ghanaian national was confronted by South Africans in a viral clip and questioned about his immigration status.

items.title: Reed to keep starting role after Dragons suffer Anzac Day belting
items.description: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

items.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
items.description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.

items.title: Hundreds of Congolese refugees voluntarily repatriated from Burundi
items.description: Hundreds of Congolese refugees living at the Busuma camp in Burundi have been voluntarily repatriated.

items.title: Italy tests R1 robotic museum guide in Turin’s historic Palazzo Madama
items.description: Italy is trialling the R1 robot guide at Turin’s Palazzo Madama to ease crowding and improve access, explaining centuries-old artworks to visitors.

items.title: Germany unveils first-ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
items.description: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

items.title: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
items.description: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

items.title: Trump administration may expand refugee programme for white South Africans
items.description: The US president has repeatedly made false claims of a genocide being carried out against minority white Afrikaners.

items.title: News24 | Iran’s new supreme leader seriously wounded, delegates power to Revolutionary Guards
items.description: Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, was seriously wounded in the US-Israeli airstrike that killed his father and predecessor, Ali Khamenei, but is mentally sharp, the New York Times reported on Thursday.

items.title: Peter Thiel’s dystopia, Milei’s utopia
items.description: There is ideological affinity between Milei and Thiel. Both of them fall under the category of anarcho-capitalist libertarians who see socialism and the decadence of Western society as major threats to humanity. Leer más

items.title: Hours before Thiel visit, Milei bans press from Casa Rosada over ‘espionage’ complaint
items.description: Argentina’s government on Thursday barred accredited press from entering the Casa Rosada as part of a “preventative” measure related to alleged “espionage” complaint; Decision, cheered by Javier Milei on social media, came hours before the head of state hosted controversial US tech entrepreneur Peter Thiel at government house. Leer más

items.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
items.description: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

items.title: UK-US ties tested: Britain courts Trump with King Charles
items.description: On a state visit to the US, the British monarch is being deployed to help steady increasingly strained US-UK relations. Can King Charles help repair the damaged so-called "special relationship?"

items.title: Milei's culture secretary heckled at opening of 50th Buenos Aires book fair
items.description: Culture Secretary Leanardo Cifelli heckled and jeered during speech at inauguration of Buenos Aires International Book Fair; Protesters criticise government, boo and whistle president's name. Leer más

items.title: Europe’s markets watchdog warns cyber threats are growing as AI speeds up risks
Hope in Gaza as 300 couples get married at one time
items.description: The 300 couples were selected in a draw of nearly 2,000 people to be a part of the joint celebration.

items.title: Armed groups stage simultaneous attacks across Mali
items.description: Armed groups have attacked Mali’s capital, its main airport, and several locations across the country.

items.title: Joy and desire for change as Gaza’s Deir el-Balah holds elections
items.description: Deir el-Balah residents vote for first time since 2006 despite destruction, displacement and genocide.

items.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
items.description: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

items.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
items.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

items.title: South Africa suspends police chief over corruption case
items.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa on Thursday suspended the national police chief implicated in a $20 million health tender scandal that has rocked the country and drawn in other officers.

items.title: Inside Chernobyl is a toxic world where frogs have turned black
items.description: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

items.title: India news: Raghav Chadha, 6 other AAP MPs join BJP
items.description: The exit of seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs has dented the party's strength in the upper house of parliament, sparking fears of political instability. DW has more.

items.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
items.description: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

items.title: U.S. won't renew Iranian, Russian oil waivers: Bessent
items.description: Bessent’s statements come as the world is on edge over the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran, and global energy markets have been ensnarled by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
items.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.

items.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
items.description: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

items.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
items.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

items.title: Rich Latin Americans are descending on Uruguay’s beach haven to party
items.description: Punta del Este is becoming a top destination for those looking to host lavish celebrations. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Several injuries after two trains in Denmark crash ‘head-on’
items.description: Two trains ‌collided in Denmark, injuring between five and 10 people, local emergency services said.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
items.description: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

items.title: Taliban Government says Afghans in Qatar can return 'with full confidence'
items.description: A U.S. State Department spokesperson said that moving Afghans at the Qatar camp “to a third country is a positive resolution that provides safety for these remaining people to start a new life outside of Afghanistan while upholding the safety and security of the American people”

items.title: After years of avoidance, Trump to attend first White House press dinner
items.description: Professional organisations call on attendees to 'speak forcefully' at annual event, which will not feature a comedian.

items.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
items.description: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

items.title: Behind Nigeria's murky coup plot
items.description: What court papers and sources close to the investigation have told the BBC about the scope of the plan.
items.source: the money, the prayers and a Nollywood arrest

items.title: Colombia, Venezuela hold  key talks in post-Maduro visit
items.description: Colombia and Venezuela announced military cooperation to combat border "mafias" during President Gustavo Petro's visit to Caracas. Petro is the first leader to travel to Venezuela after Nicolas Maduro's removal.

items.title: Viktor Orbán steps back from Parliament after landslide loss, vows to rebuild Hungary’s ’national side’
items.description: Mr. Orbán said his party’s caucus in parliament would be “radically transformed” following the election loss, and that he would not take his seat

items.title: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
items.description: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

items.title: Paredes penalty ensures Superclásico seniority for Boca
items.description: While there are few frills in this current Boca team, they are tough to break down. Leandro Paredes proved up to the challenge as Xeneize secure victory in Núñez and bragging rights over River. Leer más

items.title: Brazil blocks prediction platforms, tightens rules to curb 'bet-like' products
France voices concern about the spread of Islamist extremism in Sahel
items.description: Military regimes in Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger forced the withdrawal of French troops involved in the fight against militants in the region.

items.title: Police arrest demonstrators at pro-Palestine rally in Berlin
items.description: Video footage shared on social media shows two women being arrested at a pro-Palestine demonstration in Berlin.

items.title: Attacks on Bamako a 'dramatic setback' for Malian government
items.description: The coordinated attacks on Mali's capital of Bamako represent a "dramatic setback" for the junta-led government, said Andrew Lebovich, Research Fellow with Clingendael's Conflict Research Unit. Lebovich said the attacks also showed how tenuous the security situation in Mali is with authorities along with their Russian partners struggling to prevent the attacks in and around the capital.

items.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keep Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive
items.description: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

items.title: Russian strikes kill several in Ukraine's Dnipro
items.description: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro.

items.title: US chipmakers hit record highs as Intel turbocharges AI rally
EU considers helping with West Asia energy infrastructure to bypass conflict zones
items.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said on April 25, 2026 that EU is ready to work with Persian Gulf countries for new projects conveying energy to global markets that wouldn't be held hostage to war or geopolitical strife

items.title: Palestinian local elections give some Gazans a chance to vote for the first time in years
items.description: The West Bank-based Palestinian Authority hopes the symbolic inclusion of the Gazan city ⁠of Deir al-Balah will help reinforce its claim to authority over the war-torn territory, from where it was ousted by Hamas in 2007

items.title: News24 | Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
items.description: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smouldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.
